body.title: Log PROC_QUEUE_DRAIN and scheduler habit results persistently
body.result: log_anomaly on error-shaped scheduler results; stale-reset warning in worker_foreman; NE-empty escalation includes last 3 scheduler ticks from TWM
body.description:
PROC_QUEUE_DRAIN returns 'no sprint tickets' on every 30-min tick — but the result only lives in TWM for 5 minutes then expires. A human reading the channel 6 minutes later sees only the opaque NE 'produced no result' message. This hid a status-filter bug for 5 weeks (2026-04-08 to 2026-05-12).

Three logging gaps to close:
1. SchedulerSource._call_tool() should log.warning() when any habit returns an error-shaped result ('no sprint tickets', 'error:', '[ERROR]'). This persists to the instance log file.
2. launch_next_worker() should log.warning() whenever it resets an in_progress ticket (the pile-on was silent — no channel message, no log).
3. NE 'produced no result' message in coa.py should include the last scheduler tick results: 'last ticks: PROC_QUEUE_DRAIN=no sprint tickets, PROC_WORKER_FOREMAN=igor goal active' — so the opaque verdict becomes diagnostic.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources.py (SchedulerSource._call_tool), wild_igor/igor/tools/worker_foreman.py (launch_next_worker reset path), wild_igor/igor/cognition/coa.py (NE empty branch)
**Design rules:** docs-live-in-code for coa.py NE branch
**Scope boundary:** IN: add log.warning() at 3 sites, enhance NE-empty channel message with last-tick summary. OUT: new tables, new metrics, dashboard.
**Test plan:** no unit test needed — observable: NE-empty channel message now includes scheduler tick results; Igor log file contains warning lines when PROC_QUEUE_DRAIN returns empty.
